methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Valar Atomics

data.narrative:
Fuse Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Alan Chang and Charles Orr, currently valued at $200M+ post-money following $78M in total capital raised. The nuclear energy sector comp universe comprises 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $200M Series A post-money valuation, yielding an implied forward valuation of $400M. This methodology captures typical venture advancement patterns for mid-stage frontier technology companies. Peer-median valuation could not be applied due to insufficient comparable data points at Fuse's stage and valuation tier within nuclear energy; while 7 same-sector peers exist, only one operates at an adjacent stage and two within a similar valuation band—below the minimum threshold of three comps needed for reliable cross-sectional analysis.

The triangulation yields a median valuation of $400M, with a reasonable range spanning $340M to $460M. This single-method support reflects meaningful constraint in the analysis: the scarcity of sufficiently narrow precedents in Series A nuclear energy limits confidence in peer-based anchoring. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the named peer set, capital requirements to next inflection, and sector-level momentum in advanced reactor financing. The $400M median should be treated as illustrative pending development of richer comparable transaction data.
